docs.title: 🏠 Welcome to my dwl Config
docs.text: A heavily customized, plug-and-play dynamic tiling Wayland compositor (dwm for Wayland). This configuration features a fully patched dwl build enabling Waybar integration with workspaces/tags, a dual Catppuccin theme setup, and custom utility scripts.

docs.title: ✨ Highlights
docs.text:
• Patched IPC: Includes dwl-ipc-unstable-v2, wlr-layer-shell, and wlr-output-power-management patches to enable full Waybar communication.
  • Waybar Integration: 11 persistent tags, active window title, layout indicator, and custom modules.
  • Dual Theming: Easily switch between Catppuccin Macchiato (dark) and Latte (light) Waybar themes.
  • Dynamic Gaps: 15px window gaps with a simple keybind toggle.

docs.title: 📸 Gallery
docs.text: Here are various screenshots of the dwl setup in action.

docs.title: 📦 Installation
docs.text:
Because dwl requires custom patches and protocol files to work with Waybar, the standard AUR package (dwl-git) will fail to build or will build without Waybar support.

To bypass this, we use a custom PKGBUILD that correctly copies your pre-patched config.h and config.mk into the build environment.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Core: - wlroots-git (or wlroots0.19) - wayland-protocols - xorg-xwayland (Optional, for X11 apps)

Bar & Utilities: - waybar - rofi - wbg (Wallpaper setter) - swaync (Notification center) - lxterminal

docs.title: Building with the Custom PKGBUILD
docs.text:
1. Clone this repository:

    git clone https://codeberg.org/WgpArch/dwl.git ~/.config/dwl

  2. Navigate to the dwl source directory:

    cd ~/.config/dwl/dwl

  3. Run makepkg using your custom PKGBUILD. Because the PKGBUILD is designed to look for config.h in the parent directory, it will pull in your patched configuration automatically:

    makepkg -si

(If you use an AUR helper like trizen, you can run trizen -S dwl-git, and when it prompts you to edit the PKGBUILD, delete the contents and paste the custom PKGBUILD provided in this repo.)

  1. Ensure scripts are executable:
    chmod +x ~/.config/dwl/scripts/*

docs.title: ⌨️ Keybinds
docs.text: Here are the default keybindings for this dwl setup. The main modifier key is Super (Windows key).

docs.title: 🖥️ Programs & System
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + Return Open Terminal (lxterminal) Super + P Open App Launcher (rofi sleek-dark theme) Super + B Open Firefox Super + Shift + Q Close focused window Super + Shift + C Quit dwl Super + Shift + S Take screenshot (custom script)

docs.title: 🔍 Focus & Movement
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + J / K Focus next/previous window Super + I / D Increase/Decrease number of master windows Super + H / L Shrink/Expand master area (mfact) Super + Shift + Return Zoom (move window to master area) Super + Tab View previously selected tags

docs.title: 🗂️ Tags (Workspaces)
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + [1-9] View tag 1-9 Super + Ctrl + [1-9] Toggle view of tag 1-9 Super + Shift + [1-9] Move window to tag 1-9 Super + 0 View all tags

docs.title: 📐 Layout & Gaps
docs.text: Keybind Action Super + T Set Tiling layout Super + E Set Floating layout Super + M Set Monocle layout Super + Space Cycle layouts Super + Shift + Space Toggle floating for active window Super + F Toggle fullscreen Super + G Toggle gaps on/off

docs.title: 🔊 Media & System Keys
docs.text: Keybind Action Volume Up/Down Volume 5% +/- (wpctl) Mute Toggle audio mute Brightness Up/Down Backlight +/- 30 (brightnessctl)

docs.title: 🛠️ Patching & Building dwl
docs.text: Vanilla dwl does not support Waybar or overlay layers natively. To get Waybar to display tags (workspaces), the active layout, and the window title, you must patch dwl before compiling it.

docs.title: Required Protocols/Patches
docs.text:
You need to add three protocol XML files to your dwl source directory (specifically inside a protocols/ folder) and ensure the Makefile generates the headers for them:

  1. dwl-ipc-unstable-v2.xml: This is the most critical patch. It creates an IPC (Inter-Process Communication) interface so dwl can broadcast its state (active tags, layout, focused window) to external bars like Waybar.
  2. wlr-layer-shell-unstable-v1.xml: Allows programs (like Waybar or Rofi) to create overlay layers on top of normal windows. Without this, your bar might hide behind applications.
  3. wlr-output-power-management-unstable-v1.xml: Used for idle management and screen DPMS (turning off the screen after inactivity).

docs.title: Applying the Patches
docs.text:
1. Clone the dwl source:

    git clone https://codeberg.org/dwl/dwl.git
cd dwl

  2. Create the protocols directory and move the XML files:

    mkdir protocols
# Copy the 3 .xml files into this directory

  3. Edit the Makefile: You must instruct the build system to compile these protocols into C headers and source files. Add the protocol generation rules to your Makefile so make knows how to turn the XML files into .h and .c files that dwl.c can understand.

  4. Edit dwl.c: You must #include the generated protocol headers at the top of dwl.c, set up the global variables for the IPC manager, and hook the broadcast functions into dwl's main event loop (output layout change, focus change, etc.).

Note: The exact lines to add to Makefile and dwl.c can be complex. It is highly recommended to refer to the patched files provided in this repository's dwl/dwl/ directory as a reference for where these hooks go.

docs.title: 🎨 Look & Feel
docs.text: This dwl config uses a clean, dark aesthetic with dynamic gaps and custom border colors, heavily inspired by the dwm community standards.

docs.title: General Layout
docs.text:
• Layout: Tiling (default), Floating, Monocle.
  • Gaps: 15px (gappx), toggled with Super + G.
  • Border Size: 2px.

docs.title: Colors
docs.text: The colors are defined in config.h using hex values: - Root Background: #222222 (Dark Grey) - Border (Unfocused): #5588aa (Muted Steel Blue) - Border (Focused): #ffffff (White) - Border (Urgent): #ff0000 (Red)

docs.title: Autostart
docs.text: Applications are spawned automatically via the autostart array in config.h: - Wallpaper: wbg (Sets Anime dress wallpaper) - Waybar: Launches with config2.jsonc and style2.css - Network: nm-applet - Notifications: swaync

docs.title: Input Devices
docs.text:
• Keyboard: Repeat rate 25, delay 600. Layout pl.
  • Trackpad: Tap-to-click, tap-and-drag, and disable-while-typing enabled. 2-finger scroll.

docs.title: 📊 Waybar & Scripts
docs.text: This setup uses a custom Waybar positioned at the top center, communicating with dwl via the dwl-ipc-unstable-v2 protocol. It features dual Catppuccin themes and custom modules.

docs.title: Left
docs.text:
• Custom App Menu: Arch Linux icon. Opens Rofi (sleek-dark theme).
  • dwl/tags: Displays 11 persistent workspace tags with active/urgent indicators.
  • Tray: System app tray.

docs.title: Center
docs.text:
• Clock: Date, time, and calendar tooltip.
  • Custom Weather: Live weather data via Python script.

docs.title: Right
docs.text:
• Custom Player: 🎵 Current media playback.
  • Pulseaudio: Volume control.
  • Battery: Battery percentage and status.
  • CPU & Memory: System usage monitors.
  • Custom Notification: SwayNC integration.
  • Custom Power: Power button. Opens Rofi power menu.

docs.title: 🎨 Dual Theming
docs.text:
This config includes two Waybar themes to match your mood or ambient lighting: 1. Catppuccin Macchiato (Dark): Default theme (macchiato.css). 2. Catppuccin Latte (Light): Light theme (latte.css).

To switch themes, simply change the CSS file launched in your dwl config.h autostart array, or run waybar manually with the -s flag pointing to the desired .css file.

docs.title: 📜 Key Scripts
docs.text:
Located in ~/.config/dwl/scripts/:

Script Description screenshot_copy.sh Screenshot utility using grim/slurp that copies to clipboard. rofi_powermenu Logout, reboot, and shutdown Rofi menu.
